content.cn_text: 4. 你将听到一个关于音乐研究的讲座。首先，你有一些时间来查看
content.en_text: 4. You will hear a talk on research into music. First, you have some time to look at
the questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 今天我们将深入探讨英国音乐迷人的历史及其演变
content.en_text: Today we are going to delve into the fascinating history of music in Britain and the evolution

content.cn_text: 它在18世纪和19世纪期间所经历的一切。在
content.en_text: that it experienced throughout the duration of the 18th and 19th centuries. During the

content.cn_text: 18世纪下半叶，英国经历了一场工业革命，在此期间，其制造业
content.en_text: 2nd half of the 18th century Britain experienced an industrial revolution during which its manufacturing

content.cn_text: 从手工生产方法向机器使用发生了重大转变。
content.en_text: industry underwent a major transition from hand production methods to the use of machinery.

content.cn_text: 工业的突然繁荣吸引了成千上万的人涌入城市，在那里他们知道
content.en_text: This sudden boom in the industry grew thousands of people into the cities, where they knew

content.cn_text: 他们将能够在工厂找到工作，操作或维护机器。
content.en_text: they'd be able to find jobs in the factories, working with or maintaining the machinery.

content.cn_text: 这种突然涌入城市的人口不仅来自周边农村
content.en_text: This sudden influx of people into the cities did not just come from the surrounding countryside

content.cn_text: 也来自其他贫穷国家，以寻求更好的生活。在1800年至1900年间，人口
content.en_text: but also from other poorer nations in search of a better life. Between 1800 and 1900 the population

content.cn_text: 英格兰的人口增加了四倍。这种来自不同国家和地区的大规模移民
content.en_text: of England increased by four times. This mass immigration from different nations and areas

content.cn_text: 导致了各种文化影响的交汇融合，形成了一种新的
content.en_text: of the country resulted in a variety of cultural influences meeting and mixing to form a new

content.cn_text: 更加多元的文化。伴随着这种新文化，也诞生了一种新的音乐风格。在工业
content.en_text: more diverse culture. With this new culture came a new style of music. Prior to the industrial

content.cn_text: 革命之前，音乐最初是为了反映艰苦的劳动密集型生活而兴起的
content.en_text: revolution, music had originally been risen to reflect the hard labour-intensive life

content.cn_text: 在各种行业中，当时所有的工作都必须手工完成。大多数人居住在
content.en_text: in all kinds of industries when all work had to be done by hand. Most people resided in

content.cn_text: 小型农村社区，他们的生活围绕着农业。普通人的生活
content.en_text: small rural communities where their lives revolved around farming. Life for the average

content.cn_text: 很艰难，因为收入微薄，营养不良和疾病很常见。
content.en_text: person was difficult, as incomes were meager and malnourishment and disease were common.

content.cn_text: 尽管人们国籍不同，音乐流派多样，
content.en_text: Despite the difference in nationalities of the people and the variety of musical genres,

content.cn_text: 所有的音乐都共享一种共同的语言：英语。由于许多人不会写字，
content.en_text: all of the music shared one common language. English. As many were unable to write, the

content.cn_text: 歌曲是人们表达他们对
content.en_text: songs were a means for the people to give voice to their experiences and feelings about

content.cn_text: 他们艰苦繁重工作的经历和感受的方式。这段时期工人创作的大部分音乐
content.en_text: their hard and laborious jobs. Most of the music written by the workers during this period

content.cn_text: 由于文盲和时间的流逝而失传。
content.en_text: has been lost as a result of illiteracy and the wear of time.

content.cn_text: 现在你们可能会问，这些小型音乐作品是如何形成的。它们的根源
content.en_text: Now you may be asking yourselves how these small musical works were formed. The roots of

content.cn_text: 大多数团体都可以追溯到工人阶级中较年轻的成员，
content.en_text: most of these groups can mostly be traced back to the younger members of the working classes,

content.cn_text: 他们可能在反抗与上层阶级相比劣势的生活和工作条件。
content.en_text: who were perhaps rebelling against the inferiority of their living and working conditions compared

content.cn_text: 与上层阶级相比。通常，年轻的表演者会开创这些音乐潮流，他们通常
content.en_text: to those of the upper classes. Young performers would often leave these musical trends, usually

content.cn_text: 拥有唱歌或演奏特定手工乐器的天赋。随着时间
content.en_text: possessing a great talent for singing or playing particular handmade instruments. As time

content.cn_text: 进入19世纪，这些音乐团体声名鹊起，并会在街头
content.en_text: progressed into the 19th century, these musical groups had gained much notoriety and would

content.cn_text: 举行小型表演供所有人观看。也许正是他们盲目的乐观
content.en_text: hold small performances in the street for all to watch. It was perhaps their blind optimism

content.cn_text: 和叛逆精神使他们深受观众的欢迎，这些观众通常
content.en_text: and rebellious spirit that led them to become so popular with their audiences, which were

content.cn_text: 由最贫困的人组成。尽管上层阶级努力制止
content.en_text: usually made up of the poorest of the poor. Despite efforts by the upper classes to put an end

content.cn_text: 这些音乐传统，它们继续获得人气并蓬勃发展。到19世纪70年代后期，
content.en_text: to these musical traditions, they continued to gain popularity and flourish. By the late 1870s,

content.cn_text: 它们已经超越了社会阶层的障碍，进入了中产阶级。
content.en_text: they had evolved past the barrier of the social echelons and gained access to the middle class.

content.cn_text: 表演在村庄大厅等合适的场所举行，有些甚至
content.en_text: Performances were held in proper establishment such as village halls and some were even

content.cn_text: 需要购票入场，观众付费观看。这些团体创作的音乐
content.en_text: ticketed with audiences paying a fee for the privilege of watching. Music produced by these groups

content.cn_text: 被更好地记录下来，其中一些至今仍保存在博物馆中。这些团体的音乐可能
content.en_text: were better recorded and some is still preserved today in museums. The music of these groups may

content.cn_text: 在今天已不存在，但如果你仔细观察，其影响依然清晰可见。例如，在
content.en_text: not exist in the present day, but its influences are still clear if you look closely enough. In

content.cn_text: 古典文化中，许多主题和戏剧表演仍然基于
content.en_text: classical culture, for example, many of the themes and theatrical performances are still based

content.cn_text: 工业革命之前和之后工人阶级的生活和经历。
content.en_text: on the lives and experiences of the working classes, both prior to and following the industrial

content.cn_text: 革命。例如，维克多·雨果的小说《悲惨世界》（原文误读为“Lame is Arab”）是一部关于
content.en_text: revolution. The novel entitled, Lame is Arab by Hugh Victor Hugo, for example, is a novel about

content.cn_text: 法国工人阶级的小说，至今仍广受欢迎。当时音乐的原始录音
content.en_text: the French working classes and is still widely popular today. The original recordings of the music

content.cn_text: 现在由于狂热收藏家和热心粉丝的需求而变得非常有价值。
content.en_text: risen during these periods are now hugely valuable due to the demand from avid collectors and enthusiastic

content.cn_text: 其中许多录音是公共所有并在博物馆展出。然而，
content.en_text: fans. Many of these recordings are publicly owned and displayed in museum exhibits. However,

content.cn_text: 一部分仍是私人所有，偶尔会在拍卖会上出售，价格常常达到
content.en_text: a selection are still privately owned and occasionally appear for sale in auctions, often reaching

content.cn_text: 数十万。好了，今天的讲座就到这里，
content.en_text: prices in the hundreds of thousands. Well, that just about wraps the lecture up for today,

content.cn_text: 所以如果大家有什么问题，第四部分到此结束。你现在有半分钟的时间检查
content.en_text: so if anyone has any questions, that is the end of section 4. You now have half a minute to check

content.cn_text: 你的答案。
content.en_text: your answers.

groups.desc:
Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.
groups.title: Questions 31-40.

items.title: During the Industrial Revolution people moved to cities to work in the 31. __________.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第31题问在工业革命期间人们搬到城市是为了在什么地方工作。原文提到“thousands of people into the cities, where they knew they'd be able to find jobs in the factories”，明确指出是“factories”（工厂）。

items.title: In the 1850s, the culture was influenced greatly by 32. __________ from different countries.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第32题询问1850年代文化受什么影响。原文在提到1800年至1900年间人口增长四倍后，接着说“This mass immigration from different nations and areas of the country resulted in a variety of cultural influences meeting and mixing”，明确指出是“mass immigration”（大规模移民）带来了文化影响。

items.title: Originally music reflected the work life of different 33. __________ in those days.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第33题询问最初音乐反映了当时不同行业的什么生活。原文提到“music had originally been risen to reflect the hard labour-intensive life in all kinds of industries”，明确了音乐反映的是“industries”（行业）中的生活。

items.title: Different songs were created in the same 34. __________, although people were from a variety of nations.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第34题询问尽管人们来自不同国家，但不同歌曲是用同一种什么语言创作的。原文指出“all of the music shared one common language. English.”，强调了共同的“language”（语言）。

items.title: The songs written by the workers during this period mainly came from their feelings about harsh 35. __________.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第35题询问这段时期工人创作的歌曲主要来源于他们对严酷什么工作的感受。原文提到“songs were a means for the people to give voice to their experiences and feelings about their hard and laborious jobs”，明确指出是关于“jobs”（工作）。

items.title: The musical trends were led by 36. __________ performers.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第36题询问音乐潮流是由什么表演者引领的。原文提到“traced back to the younger members of the working classes”和“Young performers would often leave these musical trends”，明确指出是“younger members”和“Young performers”（年轻的表演者）。

items.title: During this period, some musical groups had expanded their popularity among 37. __________.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第37题询问在此期间，一些音乐团体在哪些人中扩大了知名度。原文提到“led them to become so popular with their audiences, which were usually made up of the poorest of the poor”，表明他们深受“audiences”（观众）的欢迎，而这些观众主要是“poorest of the poor”（最贫困的人）。因此，“audiences”或“the poor”都是正确答案。

items.title: In the late 1870s, the 38. __________ came into contact with these flourishing musical traditions.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第38题询问在19世纪70年代后期，哪个阶层开始接触这些蓬勃发展的音乐传统。原文明确指出“By the late 1870s, they had evolved past the barrier of the social echelons and gained access to the middle class”，即进入了“middle class”（中产阶级）。

items.title: These musical genres still exert an influence on 39. __________ culture.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第39题询问这些音乐流派至今仍对什么文化产生影响。原文提到“In classical culture, for example, many of the themes and theatrical performances are still based on the lives and experiences of the working classes”，明确指出是对“classical culture”（古典文化）有影响。

items.title: Enthusiastic fans are still collecting and keeping the 40. __________ of music from those days.
items.analysis: 答案解析：第40题询问热心粉丝仍在收集和保存那些年代的什么。原文提到“The original recordings of the music risen during these periods are now hugely valuable due to the demand from avid collectors and enthusiastic fans. Many of these recordings are publicly owned...”，明确指出是“recordings”（录音）。

groups.source_collect:
Complete the notes below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.
History of Music in Britain
In the 18th century
• During the Industrial Revolution people moved to cities to work in the $31$.
• In the 1850s, the culture was influenced greatly by $32$ from different countries.
• Originally music reflected the work life of different $33$ in those days.
• Different songs were created in the same $34$, although people were from a variety of nations.
• The songs written by the workers during this period mainly came from their feelings about harsh $35$.
• Some small musical works were composed by people who were protesting against difficult living conditions.
• The musical trends were led by $36$ performers.
In the 19th century
• During this period, some musical groups had expanded their popularity among $37$.
• In the late 1870s, the $38$ came into contact with these flourishing musical traditions.
• Some music produced by these groups is still preserved in museums.
Nowadays
• These musical genres still exert an influence on $39$ culture.
• Enthusiastic fans are still collecting and keeping the $40$ of music from those days.
